dev_plans.content: Lightweight in-pipeline per-ethnicity recall gap computed directly from the validation logits each epoch. After the validation pass, the probe takes (y_true, y_prob, ethnicity_array) and evaluates the model at a single shared threshold fixed once and for all on the first epoch's val set (threshold = argmax macro-F1 over a 0.01 grid, or 0.5 if no grid is run). It then computes per-ethnicity TPR/recall, precision, and F1, the equal-opportunity difference EOD = max_group_recall - min_group_recall, and average odds difference AOD = mean(|TPR_g - TPR_ref| + |FPR_g - FPR_ref|)/2 over groups. Implementation: (1) in the training loop's on_validation_end hook, stack all val predictions and the matching ethnicity column (already present in mimic metadata); (2) mask per unique ethnicity label; (3) for each group compute TP/FP/FN/TN at the shared threshold; (4) emit a CSV row (epoch, group, recall, precision, f1, n) and a summary row (epoch, EOD, AOD, threshold_used). The probe produces a per-epoch CSV log plus two matplotlib time-series: per-group recall curves overlaid, and EOD-over-epochs. No external fairness library — pure numpy/pandas so it stays portable and debuggable. Ethnicity groups with fewer than 30 val samples are pooled into an 'other' bucket to avoid noisy per-group estimates.
dev_plans.metric: Equal-opportunity difference (EOD) = max per-ethnicity recall - min per-ethnicity recall at the shared decision threshold, computed on the validation set each epoch (float in [0,1], lower is better).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — Hardt et al. 2016 and Fairlearn's 'less than 5 percentage-point TPR gap' is the commonly cited bar for a single threshold to be considered equal-opportunity-fair; mimic race-AUROC is inherently near-fair so the operating-point gap should be reachable with a shared threshold if calibration is honest.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — a 10-point TPR gap is the looser bar where the model is still clinically usable across groups and matches the 'per-group recall gap < 0.1' healthy line in the probe design; beyond this the orchestrator should stop chasing and instead switch levers (e.g. group-aware thresholding).

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous probe with bootstrap confidence intervals on the per-ethnicity recall gap and a threshold selected with group-aware uncertainty. Each epoch, on the validation set: (1) compute the shared threshold T* that maximizes macro-F1 on a 0.01 grid over the pooled val set; (2) at T* compute per-ethnicity recall_g and precision_g; (3) run the nonparametric stratified bootstrap (B=2000 resamples, resampling within each ethnicity stratum to preserve group proportions) to get a 95% CI on each recall_g and on EOD = max_g recall_g - min_g recall_g; (4) compute AOD with the same bootstrap; (5) also compute per-group AUROC and AUPRC with DeLong-style bootstrap CIs as a secondary diagnostic (not headline). The probe flags a 'true gap' only if the lower bound of the EOD 95% CI exceeds 0.05. Implementation uses sklearn.metrics.roc_curve/auc for AUROC and a hand-rolled stratified bootstrap (numpy default_rng(seed=epoch) for reproducibility). Outputs per epoch: a JSON {epoch, T*, per_group: {group: recall, recall_ci_low, recall_ci_high, precision, f1, auroc, auroc_ci, auprc, auprc_ci}, EOD, EOD_ci_low, EOD_ci_high, AOD, AOD_ci}. Also writes a markdown table per epoch for human review. This plan explicitly rejects per-group AUROC as the PASS/FAIL metric — recall-at-threshold is the operating-point behavior that actually drives clinical decisions.
dev_plans.metric: 95% bootstrap lower bound of the equal-opportunity difference EOD (max-group recall - min-group recall) at the shared threshold, computed on the validation set each epoch (float in [0,1], lower is better; using the CI lower bound penalizes noisy estimates and only counts a gap as real if it survives resampling).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — the lower bound of the EOD 95% CI below 0.05 means we cannot reject equality-of-opportunity at the 5-percentage-point bar; this is the strict bar because it controls for bootstrap noise so a noisy 0.04 point estimate won't pass if its CI spills above.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — if the EOD CI lower bound sits below 0.10 we treat the gap as clinically tolerable even if not formally fair; this mirrors the 10-point TPG heuristic from Chen et al. 2023 for clinical risk models and stops the orchestrator from over-optimizing on a noisy fairness signal.

dev_plans.content: Library-based probe using Fairlearn's MetricFrame and equalized-odds / demographic-parity reducers, plus AIF360's BinaryLabelDatasetMetric for cross-checking. Each epoch, after the validation pass, the probe: (1) builds a pandas DataFrame with columns [y_true, y_prob, y_pred_shared_threshold, ethnicity]; (2) wraps it in a Fairlearn MetricFrame with sensitive_features=ethnicity and computes per_group metrics for recall, precision, f1, selection_rate, and false_positive_rate using sklearn.metrics functions; (3) pulls difference_over_all_other_models (max-min) for recall -> EOD and for FPR -> AOD component, and ratio for recall as a secondary diagnostic; (4) optionally invokes Fairlearn's ThresholdOptimizer (objective='balanced accuracy', constraints='demographic_parity' OR 'equalized_odds') on the val logits to produce a post-hoc fair threshold map and logs the EOD the fair post-processor would achieve — this is a what-if diagnostic, NOT applied to the model under test; (5) dumps a per-epoch artifact via fairlearn dashboard (or a matplotlib panel if dashboard is unavailable): per-group bar chart of recall + a line chart of EOD over epochs. The MetricFrame API keeps the code ~30 lines and handles empty groups gracefully. Per-group AUROC/AUPRC are computed with sklearn and appended to the same JSON row but explicitly labeled 'secondary — not the headline'. Ethnicity groups with <30 samples are reported but flagged 'small_n'.
dev_plans.metric: Fairlearn MetricFrame difference_over_all_other_models on recall (= max per-ethnicity recall - min per-ethnicity recall) at the shared threshold, computed on the validation set each epoch (float in [0,1], lower is better).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — Fairlearn's own documentation and tutorial examples treat a <0.05 difference-over-all-other-models on recall as the 'green' band for equalized-odds / equal-opportunity constraints; this is the strict, library-recognized bar.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — a 10-point recall spread is the library's 'yellow' band where intervention (ThresholdOptimizer or group-aware calibration) is recommended but the model is still deployable; reaching this stops the orchestrator short of the strict 0.05 target.
